taskEndTimeBeforeFirstTime: End time is before the first run.
taskExceptionHandlingTip: Skip means that when a certain application encounters an exception during operation, it will directly skip to the next one. This indicates that an error in the operation of a certain application does not affect the overall completion of the scheduled task. Abort means that when a certain application encounters an exception during operation, it will directly abort the current round of the scheduled task. This indicates that an error in the operation of a certain application affects the completion of the entire scheduled task, resulting in a failure of the scheduled task execution.
taskExecuteTimeout: When a task execution exceeds the specified time limit, the corresponding task running on the terminal will be forcibly terminated. The default is 0 minutes, which means no limit.
taskQueueEnable: If there are currently executing tasks, enabling this will place the task in a waiting queue; if disabled, the task will fail to start.
